response: The compose function is a higher-order utility that uses functools.reduce to fold a sequence of unary functions into one: it repeatedly combines two functions with the rule λx: f(g(x)), so the resulting function applies the original functions from right to left (e.g., compose(f1, f2, f3)(x) ⇒ f1(f2(f3(x)))). In short, it implements classic functional-programming function composition.
